reviews.review_description:
A MYSTERIOUS ADVENTURE THROUGH A PORTAL
The Link surprised me with its simple and truly enjoyable approach: here, everything revolves around exploration.
Armed with a scanner, you gradually reveal the walls, platforms, and the entire environment in the form of a beautiful cloud of green dots.
Each scan unveils a little more of this hidden world: walls, paths, passages, until you spot the exit—a luminous blue sphere that marks the end of the level.
The atmosphere is gentle, mysterious, almost meditative: you move forward calmly, you observe, you discover… and the magic happens. The sound design can add a touch of thrill...
30 LEVELS TO FIND THE MISSING CHILDREN
The concept is enhanced by a beautiful narrative: your mission is to find the missing children of scientists transported to this other world.
But beware: small red “links” float here and there, also visible only with the scanner. You have to avoid them, because touching them sends you back to the beginning of the level—a small challenge that doesn't break the overall serenity but adds focus and pace.
With its 30 levels to explore, The Link offers a progressive, immersive, relaxing, and discovery-filled adventure.
An experience I found charming, imaginative, and particularly enjoyable for a relaxing VR session!

reviews.review_title: Sorry to leave a bad review but...
reviews.review_description: Never had a game make me so motion sick. It also locks up when it loads levels, making it worse. And after wandering around in the dark to find a blue dome for the first 6 levels, I got bored and gave up!

I've had this game for I guess a week now, but I've yet to figure out what the point of it is outside of going through invisible-wall mazes constantly "spraying"... whatever it is coming out of the device to get some idea of your present location. 
Why? Who knows...
There's zero narrative or characters and the "gameplay" loop is aimlessly spraying and walking around (and literally nothing but that minus maybe avoiding the 'Reds') small 99% empty maps to find the exit (nothing else at all to ever see or find besides the exit) so you can go to the next and do it again for no reason..
What am I missing here? 
WHAT is the point?

***UPDATE: I spoke with the dev, who is an incredibly nice person in addition to being a talented developer. He was very responsive to input and started a fix right away. The issues below (most of which actually were just initially unclear as opposed to being fundamentally problematic) are good to go. I can't wait to keep exloring the portals and discovering links!***

This is such a great use of VR. It's incredibly original, spooky (but not so terrifying that you'll crap yourself) and intriguing. Discovering the worlds as you progress through them is such a novel concept. However, it seems like there are a few design flaws that may hinder me from playing any further. Devs, please read and respond if possible, because I'd really like to keep playing this, and not have to refund it!

1) Is saving possible? During my initial play session, I discovered the first 4 links and then exited the app. Upon opening the app again, my "link count" was reset to zero. It looks like all of my progress was reset. I couldn't find any place to manually save...

2) After selecting the first link, the voice in the training facility mentioned that entering one link takes the explorer to the subsequent link. However, every time I walk into a blue sphere, I am transported back to the facility, from which point i can select another link to find. Is this right?

3) Is there an order in which the links must be collected? During my first play session, I collected the first 4 in order. During my second play session, after I disappointingly discovered that no progress seemed to have been saved, I selected and quickly found the first link. Then, just to see what would happen, I tried selecting the 29th link. Nothing happened. I thought, 'Oh, they must have to be completed in order,' so I then selected link #3, thinking it would make me do #2 first, but it actually let me do #3. Is there any rhyme or reason to this?
This game works so well for VR and the gameplay loop is fun and pretty spooky! The only things I could ask for is faster smooth turning and a seated mode.

developer_response.body: Will update above feedback :) speed and make a set height so you can play sat or standing . Thanks for the review
